nativeRange.habitat: Coral-rich areas of clear lagoon and seaward reefs, where individuals tend to remain close to thickly branching coral for shelter.

diet.notes: Generally accepts a variety of foods including frozen mysis shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, and quality flake or pellet foods. Tends to feed primarily on zooplankton in the wild.

summary: The Black Bar Chromis is a small, peaceful schooling fish from the reefs of the Western Pacific, generally considered beginner-friendly and reef-safe. It tends to do best when kept in groups of six or more in a mature reef aquarium.
careNotes: The Black Bar Chromis, known scientifically as Pycnochromis retrofasciatus (the older name Chromis retrofasciata is still widely used in trade), is generally considered one of the more peaceful and manageable reef fish. It tends to do best when kept in groups of six or more; individuals kept alone or in very small groups have been reported to become more aggressive. Ample live rock and branching coral structure is generally advised so individuals can retreat when stressed. In the wild, this species has been documented occurring at depths of 12–120 m in coral-rich lagoon and seaward reef environments. The species is diurnal, meaning it is active during the day and rests at night. Breeding has been documented in captivity; males are known to guard and aerate demersal (bottom-laid) eggs.
breedingNotes: The Black Bar Chromis is an egg-laying species that forms distinct pair bonds during the breeding period. Eggs are demersal, meaning they are laid on and adhere to a hard substrate rather than floating freely. Males guard and aerate the eggs until they hatch. Captive breeding has been reported but is not common in the hobby.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry source. Tank size 30 gal (114 L) rounded up to 120 L nice value. Temperature 72–78°F converted to 22.2–25.6°C; averaged with FishBase range.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (6.0 cm SL), native range (Western Pacific: Indonesia to Fiji, Ryukyu Is., New Caledonia, Tonga), habitat, and wild temperature range 25.4–28.9°C. Reef2Reef wiki returned 403; BlueZoo had no usable product page; Fishkeeping World had no species-specific page.

fish.breedingNotes: Egg-laying species with distinct pair bonding; males guard and aerate demersal eggs. Captive breeding has been documented but remains uncommon.
